title: Nevada Attainable Housing Account-Affordable Housing Fee Waivers
resolved_text:
Memorandum
DATE: February 18, 2026
TO: Mayor and City Council
THROUGH: Jackie Bryant, City Manager
FROM: Elaine Wiseman, Housing Administrator
Monica Kirch, Director
DEPT: Housing and Neighborhood Development
SUBJECT: Nevada Attainable Housing Account-Affordable Housing Fee Waiver Program

The Nevada Attainable Housing Account ("NAHA") was established by the Nevada Housing Access and Attainability Act (the "Act") through Assembly Bill 540 during the 2025 Legislative Session. The Act appropriated $133 million statewide to expand affordable housing opportunities for low- and moderate-income households through three program areas:
• Development Opportunities
• Homeownership Opportunities
• Local Government Matching Fund

Although the City of Reno has waived approximately $11.8 million in sewer connection and building permit fees across multiple affordable housing developments to date, the reimbursement amounts available through NAHA are governed by the Nevada Housing Division's population-based allocation methodology and the statutory criteria established in Assembly Bill 540. Based on this, the City of Reno's total amount available for reimbursement under the Nevada Attainable Housing Account program is $2,139,983.

The Nevada Housing Division further refined the qualifying reimbursement period, allowing local jurisdictions to seek reimbursement for projects that received certificates of occupancy in Fiscal Year 2025, when the City of Reno's Sewer Enterprise Fund absorbed the actual cost.

During FY 2025, two qualifying developments came online within the City of Reno: Vintage at Redfield and Vintage at Stoker. Together, Vintage at Redfield and Vintage at Stoker represent 703 affordable housing units, for a total amount of $2,497,158 in fees waived. Under Assembly Bill 540, jurisdictions are required to provide a 50 percent match to be eligible for reimbursement through NAHA.

Accordingly, the City of Reno applied to the Nevada Housing Division requesting reimbursement in the amount of $1,248,579, which is half of the amount waived.

On February 5, 2026, the NAHA Council awarded the City of Reno the full $1,248,579 as requested. Following this award, the City of Reno will remain eligible to apply for approximately $891,404 in reimbursement from the 2026 NAHA Allocation Plan.

Housing and Neighborhood Development staff will convene internally to evaluate the future of the Nevada Attainable Housing Account-Affordable Housing Fee Waiver Program and will keep the Mayor and City Council informed.

fields.authors: Elaine Wiseman, Housing Administrator; Monica Kirch, Director
fields.subcategory: Affordable Housing / Fee Waiver / State Funding
fields.tldr: Nevada's Assembly Bill 540 created the Nevada Attainable Housing Account (NAHA), appropriating $133 million statewide for affordable housing. The City of Reno applied for reimbursement of half the fees waived for two FY 2025 affordable housing developments, totaling $1,248,579, and was awarded the full amount on February 5, 2026. Reno remains eligible for an additional ~$891,404 in the 2026 NAHA Allocation Plan. Staff will continue evaluating the program and keep Council informed.
fields.key_stats: Statewide NAHA appropriation: $133 million; Total fees waived by Reno to date: ~$11.8 million; Reno's total NAHA reimbursement eligibility: $2,139,983; FY 2025 qualifying developments: Vintage at Redfield and Vintage at Stoker (703 combined affordable units); Total fees waived for those two projects: $2,497,158; Reimbursement requested (50% match requirement): $1,248,579; Amount awarded: $1,248,579; Remaining 2026 NAHA eligibility: ~$891,404
fields.key_decisions: The NAHA Council awarded the City of Reno the full requested reimbursement of $1,248,579 on February 5, 2026. The City must provide a 50% match to qualify for NAHA reimbursement under Assembly Bill 540.
fields.action_items: Housing and Neighborhood Development staff will convene internally to evaluate the future of the NAHA-Affordable Housing Fee Waiver Program and will keep the Mayor and City Council informed; the City may apply for approximately $891,404 in reimbursement from the 2026 NAHA Allocation Plan.
